title: Errors while trying to compile for MINGW64 in Windows 10
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

I've ran these commands:
```
git clone https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys.git
cd yosys
make config-msys2-64
make
```

### Error 01

```
couldn't read file "/proc/1674/fd/63": no such file or directory
[  2%] Building kernel/yosys.o
```
Worked for me after changing this line in the Makefile:
```
TCL_VERSION ?= tcl$(shell bash -c "tclsh <(echo 'puts [info tclversion]')")
```
To:
```
TCL_VERSION ?= tcl$(shell echo "puts [info tclversion]" | tclsh)
```

### Error 02

```
[100%] Building yosys.exe
C:/msys64/mingw64/bin/../lib/gcc/x86_64-w64-mingw32/11.2.0/../../../../x86_64-w64-mingw32/bin/ld.exe: cannot find -ltcl8.6: No such file or directory
collect2.exe: error: ld returned 1 exit status
make: *** [Makefile:709: yosys.exe] Error 1
```
The Makefile has this if-else statement to define LDLIBS:
```
ifeq ($(OS), FreeBSD)
# FreeBSD uses tcl8.6, but lib is named "libtcl86"
LDLIBS += $(shell PKG_CONFIG_PATH=$(PKG_CONFIG_PATH) $(PKG_CONFIG) --silence-errors --libs tcl || echo -l$(TCL_VERSION) | tr -d '.')
else
LDLIBS += $(shell PKG_CONFIG_PATH=$(PKG_CONFIG_PATH) $(PKG_CONFIG) --silence-errors --libs tcl || echo -l$(TCL_VERSION))
endif
```
For MINGW64, I think we need the LDLIBS of the "FreeBSD" if branch:
```
LDLIBS += $(shell PKG_CONFIG_PATH=$(PKG_CONFIG_PATH) $(PKG_CONFIG) --silence-errors --libs tcl || echo -l$(TCL_VERSION) | tr -d '.')
```
This changed the ld argument -ltcl8.6 to -ltcl86 and worked for me.
code_blocks:
ifeq ($(OS), FreeBSD)
# FreeBSD uses tcl8.6, but lib is named "libtcl86"
LDLIBS += $(shell PKG_CONFIG_PATH=$(PKG_CONFIG_PATH) $(PKG_CONFIG) --silence-errors --libs tcl || echo -l$(TCL_VERSION) | tr -d '.')
else
LDLIBS += $(shell PKG_CONFIG_PATH=$(PKG_CONFIG_PATH) $(PKG_CONFIG) --silence-errors --libs tcl || echo -l$(TCL_VERSION))
endif
